citations.narrative:
During inspection, records review identified that two shipments of nonhuman primates did not maintain a record of the
health certificate issued by a licensed veterinarian for shipments dated 8 January 2025 from Louisiana to Nevada and 29
January 2025 from Florida to Maryland.
USDA regulation requires that the accepting carrier or intermediate handler shall keep and maintain a copy of the health
certificate completed as required by 2.78, tendered with each nonhuman primate transport.
Correct by 23 February 2026 for all future nonhuman primate shipments.

During records review, an animal health observation log did not indicate that the animals transported from Texas to
Louisiana were observed at minimum once every 4 hours during transportation. The original record indicated two checks
at 9AM P/T (11 AM in Central time) on 3 December 2025 and 6:20 AM (Central time) on 4 December 2025. The registrant
contacted the driver, who confirmed a weather delay in loading/unloading but the requirement is still to observe the
animals no less than every 4 hours.
Correct by 23 February 2025 and for all future transportation of nonhuman primates.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the registrant.
n

narrative:
2.77(b) 
   
Records: Carriers and intermediate handlers. 
During inspection, records review identified that two shipments of nonhuman primates did not maintain a record of the 
health certificate issued by a licensed veterinarian for shipments dated 8 January 2025 from Louisiana to Nevada and 29 
January 2025 from Florida to Maryland. 
 
USDA regulation requires that the accepting carrier or intermediate handler shall keep and maintain a copy of the health 
certificate completed as required by 2.78, tendered with each nonhuman primate transport. 
 
Correct by 23 February 2026 for all future nonhuman primate shipments. 
3.90(a) 
   
Care in transit. 
During records review, an animal health observation log did not indicate that the animals transported from Texas to 
Louisiana were observed at minimum once every 4 hours during transportation. The original record indicated two checks 
at 9AM P/T (11 AM in Central time) on 3 December 2025 and 6:20 AM (Central time) on 4 December 2025. The registrant 
contacted the driver, who confirmed a weather delay in loading/unloading but the requirement is still to observe the 
animals no less than every 4 hours. 
 
Correct by 23 February 2025 and for all future transportation of nonhuman primates. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the registrant.
